notes: Official Monarch product capture for TreasuryBench V1.
response:
From May 1 to May 31, 2026, you spent $1,870.00 on kid-related expenses in the Childcare category.

Here’s how that number came together:

For “childcare and kid-related expenses,” I used these categories: Child Care, Child Activities, and Childcare.
In May 2026, only Childcare had any spending.
Total outflow in Childcare for that period was $1,870.00.

If you want this to stay accurate going forward, here are a few quick things to check in Monarch:

Kid-related categorization

Make sure daycare, aftercare, babysitting, and summer camp are in Childcare, Child Care, or Child Activities.
Scan Uncategorized, Education, and Household for any kid-related charges and recategorize them.

Reimbursements and FSAs

If you get money back (like from a Dependent Care FSA or your employer), categorize those as income or a separate reimbursement category.
Don’t put reimbursements in the same expense category as the childcare itself, or your actual spending will look lower than it is.

Ongoing care vs activities

Use Childcare for core / ongoing care (daycare, aftercare, regular nanny).
Use Child Activities for extras (sports, classes, camps, lessons).

That setup will make it easier to see what’s fixed vs what’s more flexible in your kid spending each month.
